name: Robert Peters
bio: Robert Peters is a Democratic candidate for Illinois' 2nd Congressional District in the March 17, 2026 primary. Peters was born and raised in Hyde Park, Chicago. He was born deaf to a mother who was addicted to drugs and was placed for adoption by a social worker mother and a civil rights attorney father — experiences that have profoundly shaped his legislative work. He earned his undergraduate degree from Kansas State University. He has worked for ICIRR (Illinois Coalition for Immigrant and Refugee Rights), Chicago Votes, and Reclaim Chicago, and served as Political Director for Daniel K. Biss's gubernatorial campaign. Peters is a dues-paying member of The People's Lobby and United Working Families. He was appointed to the Illinois State Senate District 13 (Hyde Park/South Shore/Kenwood) in 2019 to fill the vacancy left by Kwame Raoul (elevated to Illinois Attorney General), and won election outright in 2020 in a contested primary (53.3% vs. 46.7% for Ken Thomas). He won reelection unopposed in 2022 and 2024 (term through January 10, 2029). He is currently the Illinois Senate Majority Whip and chairs the Senate Labor Committee. Peters was the lead Senate sponsor of the landmark SAFE-T Act (SB 2835, signed January 22, 2021), Illinois' comprehensive criminal justice reform law that abolished cash bail and enacted wide-ranging law enforcement accountability measures. He raised $1.13M in FEC receipts as of February 25, 2026 — second in fundraising in the 11-candidate IL-02 primary, behind only Donna Miller ($1.97M). Peters entered the race after incumbent Robin Kelly stepped down to run for U.S. Senate.
prior_office: Illinois State Senator, District 13 (2019–present); Senate Majority Whip; Chair, Illinois Senate Labor Committee

jacket.note: FEC data from committee C00905075 (ROBERT PETERS FOR CONGRESS). 2,188 total receipts — by far the most of any IL-02 candidate except Donna Miller, reflecting broad grassroots fundraising. ActBlue conduit total $354,121 confirms large small-dollar base. Top $7,000 donors: Leo Smith (not employed, Chicago), Rebecca Janowitz (not employed, Chicago), Aaron Collard (real estate, Hyde Park). Progressive Turnout Project (Evanston-based) gave $10,000. Sanders-affiliated Progressive Voters of America gave $5,000. FEC confirmed: $1,130,677 raised, $188,510 cash on hand as of Feb 25, 2026. FEC ID H6IL02298 confirmed to be Robert Peters for IL-02 (not a state/county race).

donors.name: Progressive Voters of America (Bernie Sanders PAC)

jacket.donors_note: Itemized donor data pending ETL import from FEC filing H6IL02298. Total confirmed via Ballotpedia/FEC aggregate.

key_votes.bill: SAFE-T Act (SB 2835) — Illinois Safety, Accountability, Fairness and Equity-Today Act
key_votes.summary: Peters was the lead Senate sponsor of Illinois' landmark criminal justice reform bill, signed by Gov. Pritzker on January 22, 2021. The law abolished cash bail (effective September 18, 2023), expanded pretrial rights, established new law enforcement accountability standards, required body cameras, and reformed use-of-force policies. It was the most significant criminal justice reform legislation in Illinois history.

key_votes.bill: Illinois Housing Justice Act — Tenant Protections / Just-Cause Eviction
key_votes.summary: Peters was a primary Senate sponsor of legislation expanding tenant protections, including just-cause eviction standards to protect renters from arbitrary displacement.

Peters worked to expand the senior property tax freeze so seniors and working-class people could stay in their homes as property values increased, particularly in the South Shore and Hyde Park areas he represents.
Voted to raise Illinois' minimum wage — part of the graduated minimum wage increase to $15/hour passed by the Illinois General Assembly in 2019.

key_votes.bill: End State Sales Tax on Groceries
key_votes.summary: Voted to eliminate the Illinois state sales tax on groceries to lower the cost of living for working families.

As someone who previously worked for ICIRR, Peters has been a consistent advocate for immigrant rights protections in the Illinois Senate, opposing ICE enforcement overreach and championing protections for immigrant communities.

career_history.highlight: Appointed 2019, elected 2020. Currently Senate Majority Whip and Chair of Senate Labor Committee. Lead Senate sponsor of the SAFE-T Act. District covers Hyde Park, South Shore, Kenwood.

career_history.org: Daniel K. Biss for Governor campaign
career_history.highlight: Directed political strategy for the progressive gubernatorial campaign of then-State Sen. Daniel Biss.
Community organizing and civic engagement focused on youth voter registration and mobilization.

career_history.org: ICIRR (Illinois Coalition for Immigrant and Refugee Rights)
career_history.highlight: Immigrant rights and advocacy work on the South Side of Chicago.
Progressive political organizing in Chicago, affiliated with The People's Lobby.

policy_platform.position: Lead architect of Illinois' SAFE-T Act (end of cash bail, law enforcement accountability). Will push at federal level to end mass incarceration, strengthen community violence prevention programs, and advocate for a fairer criminal justice system.

Supports strong tenant protections, just-cause eviction standards, affordable housing funding, and banning out-of-state corporations from buying up residential properties. Expanded senior property tax freeze.
Supports Medicare for All. Will fight Trump's Medicaid and ACA cuts. Advocates for bringing a Level 1 or Level 2 trauma center to the South Suburbs.

Current chair of IL Senate Labor Committee. Supports the PRO Act to strengthen collective bargaining rights. Voted to raise minimum wage, eliminate grocery tax. Supports equal pay protections for women and Black and Brown workers.
Will vote to ban mass deportation raids, ban ICE arrests without warrants, and oppose Trump administration immigration enforcement overreach.
Led Illinois legislation protecting abortion rights and access. Used IVF to start his own family; will fight to restore and codify reproductive rights nationally.
Will fight Trump's attacks on Social Security and Medicare; supports expanding Social Security benefits and forcing Medicare to negotiate drug prices.

social_pulse.summary: High positive sentiment among progressive activists in Illinois, Chicago Votes/People's Lobby community, and criminal justice reform advocates nationally. Named alongside Jesse Jackson Jr., Donna Miller, and Willie Preston as a top-tier IL-02 candidate by Chicago Tribune and Ballotpedia. Second in fundraising in the race. David Hogg's Leaders We Deserve endorsement gives national progressive visibility.
